content.verdict: Kamalame Cay is a 96-acre private island off the coast of Andros — the largest, least-developed island in the Bahamas. The barefoot-luxury resort sits between the world's third-largest barrier reef on one side and the bonefishing flats of Andros on the other, with just 22 villas and cottages set among coconut palms. For honeymooners seeking total seclusion without sacrificing service, Kamalame Cay is one of the Bahamas' most romantic addresses.
content.best_room: The Beach Houses — three-bedroom standalone homes directly on the white-sand beach — are the property's flagship. For couples specifically, the One-Bedroom Beach Cottages and the Honeymoon Beachfront Suite offer the same direct beach access at a lower scale. The over-water spa, reached by a long jetty, is one of the few in the Caribbean. Beach Cottage 11 is the most private, set at the quieter end of the beach.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival via Andros
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly to Andros Town from Nassau (15 minutes) or directly from Florida. A short boat transfer crosses the channel to Kamalame Cay's private dock. Check in at the colonial Great House, settle into your beachfront cottage, and have the first dinner at the Great House — fresh-caught grouper with Bahamian sides, watching the sunset over the Andros barrier reef.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Reef Snorkeling & Beach Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Kamalame's offshore reef is part of the world's third-largest barrier reef system, with extraordinary visibility and abundant marine life. Morning snorkel trip from the dock — coral gardens, parrotfish, sea turtles, occasional reef sharks. Afternoon on the deserted three-mile beach with a picnic and a cooler delivered by the staff.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Andros Blue Holes
itinerary_7_nights.description: Andros has the world's highest concentration of blue holes — vertical underwater caves cut through the limestone, both inland and on the reef. A guided trip to inland blue holes (a short drive from the boat dock) for swimming in the eerily clear, deep freshwater. The marine blue holes are accessible by boat and offer dramatic drift snorkeling for confident swimmers.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Bonefishing or Spa Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Andros is the world's bonefishing capital — vast shallow flats teeming with the silver, fast-running fish. A half-day with a Kamalame guide on a flats skiff is a quintessential Andros experience even for non-anglers. Alternative: morning at the over-water spa cabana for a couples treatment with the sound of the water below the deck. Lunch at the beach bar.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sandbar Picnic & Reef
itinerary_7_nights.description: Boat day around Kamalame's surrounding cays — drop at a deserted sandbar for a champagne lunch and swim, then late-afternoon snorkeling on the reef as the light softens. The water around Kamalame is the impossibly clear turquoise that makes the Bahamas iconic. Dinner at the Tiki Bar with the staff lighting torches as night falls.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Beach Walk & Conch Cracking
itinerary_7_nights.description: Walk the full length of Kamalame's beach — three miles of mostly empty white sand. Lunch at the beachfront restaurant with a conch cracking demonstration: Bahamian fishermen show how to extract conch from the shell and prepare conch salad with lime, hot pepper, and onion at your table. Late afternoon hammock time, sunset cocktails.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Reef Swim & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: A final morning snorkel at the house reef, breakfast at the Great House, and the short boat transfer back to Andros Town airport for the connection through Nassau. Most guests leave already planning a return — Kamalame's combination of seclusion and barefoot luxury creates strong loyalty.

caveats: Kamalame Cay is genuinely remote — Andros has limited infrastructure, no nightlife beyond the resort, and reaching the cay requires a connection through Nassau or a charter from Florida.
The property is family-friendly and not adults-only — though the scale is small and atmosphere quiet, families with children are welcomed and may share the beach and dining.
The over-water spa cabana and certain villa upgrades book quickly; some of the most desirable Beach Houses are reserved for groups, limiting couple availability in peak season.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Kamalame Cay is exceptional for honeymooners who want the private-island experience without the staffed-villa formality of the highest-end Caribbean resorts. The barefoot luxury, the over-water spa, the empty three-mile beach, and the genuine seclusion make it one of the Bahamas' most romantic addresses for couples who don't need casinos and shopping.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: November through May is the dry season with consistent weather. December through April is peak. Bonefishing is excellent year-round but March through June and October to November are considered prime. Hurricane season is August through October — Andros sees occasional weather events but is far from the typical hurricane track.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: With only 22 villas and cottages, Kamalame Cay sells out 4 to 6 months ahead for peak season. Beach Cottages and the Honeymoon Beachfront Suite are the first to go for couples. Christmas and New Year often book a year in advance.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: Kamalame Cay is not adults-only — families do visit, particularly during school breaks and bonefishing trips. The intimate scale and barefoot atmosphere mean it skews adult and couple-heavy, especially mid-week and outside school holidays.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: The One-Bedroom Beach Cottages are ideal for honeymooners — direct beach access, private outdoor showers, and the property's full barefoot-luxury character. Beach Cottage 11 is the most private. Couples wanting a splurge can book one bedroom of a Beach House for full standalone-home privacy.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Andros Town airport is the gateway — 15-minute flights from Nassau on Western Air or Bahamas Air. Kamalame arranges the boat transfer (15 minutes) on arrival. Direct charters from Florida are also possible. Total travel time from US East Coast cities is 4 to 6 hours.
